sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, the cleanest win is a more stable carb profile.
sections.subtitleText: 0.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, the cleanest win is a more stable carb profile.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 9.6g of carbs. glycemic index is where this starts to get messy. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: 9.6g of carbs. glycemic index is where this starts to get messy. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 1.6g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: 1.6g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: practical staple carb source. tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day. can be quite filling. a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible. whole-food carb package. that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: still a carb-heavy category. that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage. satiety payoff depends a lot on format. some versions are much steadier than others. can go downhill fast when heavily processed. the category has a big preparation trap.

closing.summary: Beetroot does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
